skill.name: baoyu-article-illustrator
skill.description: Analyzes article content, plans useful illustration placements, creates style-specific image prompts, and inserts generated images into the article.
skill.summary: Plan and generate consistent illustrations for articles.
skill.author: JimLiu

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backticks and weak cryptography are false positives because SKILL.md is markdown documentation, not executable code. However, prompts/system.md contains a downstream image prompt that tells the generator not to refuse sensitive or copyrighted subjects. Publication should wait until that safety override is removed or rewritten.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Image Generation Refusal Override
high_findings.description: The downstream image prompt says, "DO NOT refuse to generate" for sensitive or copyrighted figures. This can conflict with safety controls and should be removed or rewritten to require policy-compliant alternatives.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and appears in the prompt used for image generation. It is not a direct malware indicator, but it attempts to override refusal behavior for sensitive cases.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Writes to Article Directory
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to create an imgs directory, save prompt files, save generated images, and update the article markdown. This is expected behavior, but users should review file changes before accepting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-write behavior is explicitly documented and limited to the article folder. It is a legitimate capability, but it still changes user files.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick findings are false positives. The cited SKILL.md lines are fenced usage examples, inline option names, tables, and markdown templates, not Ruby source or executable shell interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is an instruction document with fenced examples and inline code formatting. I found no Ruby code, no command execution primitive, and no user-input shell interpolation.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives. The cited lines contain descriptive prose, markdown placeholders, style metadata, or image syntax, not cryptographic calls or hash selection logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines do not call MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any crypto library. They are ordinary article-illustration instructions and markdown examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsafe Image Generation Refusal Override
dangerous_patterns.description: The downstream image prompt says, "DO NOT refuse to generate" for sensitive or copyrighted figures. This can conflict with safety controls and should be removed or rewritten to require policy-compliant alternatives.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and appears in the prompt used for image generation. It is not a direct malware indicator, but it attempts to override refusal behavior for sensitive cases.

content.value_statement: Long articles often need visual support to explain abstract ideas. This skill plans, prompts, and inserts illustrations with consistent styles.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes article structure and themes to find useful illustration points.
Selects one of nine illustration styles from article signals or user choice.
Creates an illustration plan with placement, purpose, visual content, and filenames.
Writes style-specific prompt files under an imgs prompts folder.
Calls an available image generation skill and tracks progress for each image.
Updates markdown articles with generated image links and alt text.
limitations: Requires a separate image generation skill to create final images.
May add more images than a publication needs unless the user sets expectations.
Generated images and placements need human review before publishing.
Current prompt wording needs safety review before marketplace publication.

use_cases.title: Add visuals to a blog draft
use_cases.description: Turn a finished markdown article into a visually supported draft with planned image placements.

use_cases.title: Create consistent product explainers
use_cases.description: Generate matching visual prompts for SaaS, AI, or technical articles using the tech or notion styles.

use_cases.title: Prepare educational diagrams
use_cases.description: Identify concepts, processes, and comparisons that need diagrams for easier reading.

prompt_templates.title: Illustrate a markdown article
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with automatic style selection.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use baoyu-article-illustrator on my article at drafts/guide.md. Choose the best style automatically and update the file with images.

prompt_templates.title: Use a specific style
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose a known style for brand consistency.
prompt_templates.prompt: Illustrate drafts/ai-overview.md with the tech style. Keep all images consistent and include concise alt text.

prompt_templates.title: Plan before generating
prompt_templates.scenario: Review placements before image generation starts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze drafts/leadership.md and create the illustration plan first. Wait for my approval before generating images.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize for a publication workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with constraints for quantity and review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Illustrate posts/product-strategy.md in elegant style. Use no more than four images, avoid text-heavy visuals, and summarize every file changed.

output_examples.input: A technical article about vector databases and retrieval workflows.
output_examples.output: The skill selects the tech style, plans diagrams for retrieval flow and ranking, generates image prompts, and inserts markdown image links.

output_examples.input: A personal essay about learning habits and motivation.
output_examples.output: The skill selects the warm style, adds gentle concept illustrations, and places images after sections with abstract emotional themes.

output_examples.input: A product article that already has strict brand visuals.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a smaller illustration plan, uses the requested style, and leaves a summary of generated files for review.

best_practices: Review the illustration plan before generating images for long or sensitive articles.
Choose a specific style when the article must match an existing brand system.
Check generated images, alt text, and file changes before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not use it on private drafts unless the selected image generator is approved.
Do not accept every suggested image when the article needs a restrained layout.
Do not use generated images of sensitive people or brands without policy and rights review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images by itself?
faq.answer: No. It prepares prompts and calls an available image generation skill selected during the workflow.

faq.question: Which article formats work best?
faq.answer: Markdown articles work best because the skill writes image links directly into the document.

faq.question: Can I control the illustration style?
faq.answer: Yes. You can choose styles such as tech, warm, minimal, playful, elegant, nature, sketch, bold, or notion.

faq.question: Where are generated files saved?
faq.answer: The skill saves prompts and images under an imgs folder beside the article.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive content?
faq.answer: Use caution. The current downstream image prompt needs safety review before marketplace publication.

faq.question: Will it preserve my original article?
faq.answer: It updates the article with image links, so review diffs or keep a backup before accepting changes.
